Proof of concept is basically knowing that if you do these things this result is going to happen. And you know what your proof of concept is after you've got results for around three to five clients who are in a similar niche. So if you're helping plumbers get more clients with Google AdWords and you do it for one of them and they get good results, that's not a proof of concept. But if you do it with three to five of them and they get good results and you're still doing the same thing for them, boom, that's a proof of concept. A proof of concept really is knowing if I do these things to this particular person, they're going to experience this result. It's when you stop really coming up with all of these bespoke solutions. You stop really trying to pull a rabbit out of the hat for every new client you've got and you just know if I follow this process and this method which I've got, this client's going to get results. It's when your consulting business goes from really being a labor force for hire where you've got to figure out what to do and then do it to an expert business where you're just implementing a process which you know is going to work.
